scenario: Apex Logistics runs Camunda 8.8 with 800 instances in Operate. For one stuck instance the operator wants to start a new token at the Pack Shipment flow node so that activity will execute, without removing any tokens that are already running elsewhere in the same instance.
question: Which modification-mode action adds a new token to a flow node on a single process instance?

options.text: In modification mode the operator selects the target flow node in the diagram and clicks Add from its dropdown, after which the new token appears as a pending modification in the instance history
In modification mode the operator selects the target flow node and chooses Spawn worker, which immediately starts a token there and bypasses the pending modification summary entirely
The operator opens the process instance variables panel and sets a hidden activityId variable to the target flow node, prompting the broker to seed a token there on the next poll
The operator deploys a new version of the BPMN model with the target flow node marked as a start point, so the engine picks up an extra token for that flow node on existing instances

a.text: Correct. The docs describe adding a new token by selecting the flow node and clicking Add from its dropdown in modification mode; the new token is then visible as a pending modification reflected in the instance history.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no Spawn worker action in the modification flow, and added tokens are always staged as pending modifications first rather than starting immediately.

c.text: Incorrect. Tokens are not introduced by setting hidden variables; the documented mechanism is the explicit Add action on a selected flow node inside modification mode.

d.text: Incorrect. Adding a token to a single running instance is an Operate modification action; redeploying the BPMN model does not retroactively seed tokens onto existing process instances.

explanation: To add a new token, the operator enters modification mode, selects the target flow node, and clicks Add from its dropdown. The new token is then staged as a pending modification reflected in the instance history before being applied.
